# Codex `app-server` JSON-RPC — Spike Findings

Captured 2026-05-21. `codex-cli` version **0.132.0**.

The exact protocol is machine-generated by the installed CLI:
```
codex app-server generate-json-schema --out <dir>   # JSON Schema
codex app-server generate-ts          --out <dir>   # TypeScript bindings
```
Re-run these on a CLI upgrade to diff the contract. Do NOT trust an external
reference (e.g. a T3Code dump) — verify against the installed version.

## Transport

- Spawn: `codex app-server` (no extra args). stdio pipes.
- Wire: newline-delimited JSON, JSON-RPC 2.0-ish.
- **Responses omit the `jsonrpc` field** — server replies are `{"id":N,"result":{...}}`
  or `{"id":N,"error":{...}}`. Match purely by `id`.
- Client→server requests/notifications DO send `jsonrpc:"2.0"`. Server accepts that.
- Server→client requests carry `method` + `id` + `params`; reply with `{"id":N,"result":{...}}`.
- Server→client notifications carry `method` + `params`, no `id`.

## Handshake

1. `initialize` request → `{ clientInfo:{name,title,version}, capabilities:{experimentalApi:true, requestAttestation:false} }`.
   Response: `{ userAgent, codexHome, platformFamily, platformOs }`.
2. `initialized` notification (params `{}`).
3. `thread/start` request → `{ cwd, approvalPolicy?, sandbox?, model? }`.
   Response: `{ thread:{id,...}, model, approvalPolicy, sandbox, reasoningEffort, ... }`.
   Server also fires a `thread/started` notification with the same thread.
   `thread/resume` request → `{ threadId }` to resume a persisted thread.

Unprompted notifications also arrive early: `remoteControl/status/changed`,
`mcpServer/startupStatus/updated`. Treat unknown notifications as ignorable.

## A turn

`turn/start` request → `{ threadId, input:[{type:'text',text,text_elements:[]}],
approvalPolicy?, sandboxPolicy?, model?, effort? }`.
**The `turn/start` response resolves immediately** (`turn.status:'inProgress'`) —
it is NOT held until turn end. The turn streams via notifications; `turn/completed`
marks the end.

Observed notification sequence for a trivial prompt:
```
thread/status/changed      {status:{type:'active'}}
turn/started               {threadId, turn}
hook/started, hook/completed   (session/turn lifecycle hooks — ignorable noise)
item/started   userMessage     (Codex echoes the user msg as an item — skip; we emit our own)
item/completed userMessage
item/started   agentMessage    (text:'')
item/agentMessage/delta        {itemId, delta}
item/completed agentMessage    (text:'OK', phase:'final_answer')
thread/tokenUsage/updated      {tokenUsage:{total,last,modelContextWindow}}
account/rateLimits/updated
thread/status/changed      {status:{type:'idle'}}
turn/completed             {threadId, turn:{status:'completed', durationMs}}
```

## Key types (from generated bindings)

- `ServerNotification` — ~65 methods. Relevant: `item/agentMessage/delta`,
  `item/reasoning/textDelta`, `item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta`,
  `item/started`/`item/completed`, `item/commandExecution/outputDelta`,
  `item/fileChange/outputDelta`, `turn/started`/`turn/completed`,
  `turn/plan/updated`, `turn/diff/updated`, `thread/tokenUsage/updated`,
  `thread/status/changed`, `error`.
- `ServerRequest` (server→client) — approvals:
  `item/commandExecution/requestApproval`, `item/fileChange/requestApproval`,
  `item/tool/requestUserInput`, `item/permissions/requestApproval`,
  `item/tool/call`, plus legacy `applyPatchApproval`/`execCommandApproval`.
- `ThreadItem` — discriminated by `type`: `userMessage`, `agentMessage`, `plan`,
  `reasoning`, `commandExecution`, `fileChange`, `mcpToolCall`, `dynamicToolCall`,
  `webSearch`, `imageView`, `contextCompaction`, etc.
- `ReviewDecision` — approval reply: `"approved" | "approved_for_session" |
  "denied" | "timed_out" | "abort"` (+ amendment object variants).
- `AskForApproval` — `"untrusted" | "on-failure" | "on-request" | "never"` (+granular).
- `SandboxPolicy` — `{type:'dangerFullAccess'} | {type:'readOnly',networkAccess} |
  {type:'workspaceWrite',writableRoots,networkAccess,...}`.
- `ReasoningEffort` — `"none"|"minimal"|"low"|"medium"|"high"|"xhigh"`.
  SlayZone's `max` has no Codex equivalent → map `max`→`xhigh`.

## Runtime-mode → Codex policy mapping

| SlayZone runtime mode | `approvalPolicy` | `sandboxPolicy` (turn) / `sandbox` (thread) |
|-----------------------|------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| approval-required     | `untrusted`      | `{type:'readOnly',networkAccess:false}` / `read-only` |
| auto-accept-edits     | `on-request`     | `{type:'workspaceWrite',...}` / `workspace-write` |
| full-access           | `never`          | `{type:'dangerFullAccess'}` / `danger-full-access` |

## Defaults observed

- Default model: `gpt-5.5` (use `model/list` request for the live catalog;
  do not hardcode).
- Default `reasoningEffort`: `high`. Default `approvalPolicy`: `on-request`.

## Fixtures

- `simple-turn.ndjson` — server output for a trivial one-message turn
  (`approvalPolicy:'never'`, `sandbox:'workspace-write'`). One `>>` line of
  context per request omitted; this file is server→client lines only.
</content>
